symptoms.symptom_name: New-onset severe localised right temporal headache in an older adult, unrelieved by simple analgesia
symptoms.duration: constant for 5 days, worst at night
aggravating_factors: resting the head on the pillow on the affected side

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A new localised temporal headache in an adult over 50 is a cardinal feature of GCA; new headache in this age group is a recognised discriminating feature.
Headache is compatible with tension-type headache, but the new onset, severity, localisation, and refractoriness to analgesia are atypical for it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Jaw claudication — ischaemic pain and fatigue of the chewing muscles on chewing, relieved by rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Jaw claudication is among the features most strongly associated with GCA (high positive likelihood ratio) and is particularly associated with subsequent visual loss, mandating urgent evaluation and treatment.
TMJ dysfunction produces mechanical joint pain (clicking/locking), not ischaemic fatigue relieved by rest; the claudication pattern argues against a primary joint cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marked scalp tenderness over the affected temple

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Scalp tenderness reflects inflammation of scalp arteries and is a recognised supportive feature of GCA in the correct clinical context.
Marked localised scalp tenderness of this severity is not characteristic of tension headache.

symptoms.symptom_name: Constitutional features — profound fatigue, low-grade fever, unintentional weight loss (~2kg/2 weeks)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic inflammatory features (fatigue, low-grade fever, weight loss) accompany the granulomatous inflammation of GCA and support a systemic inflammatory diagnosis over a benign local headache disorder; typically associated with markedly elevated ESR/CRP.

symptoms.symptom_name: No visual disturbance or loss at presentation (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact vision does NOT reduce the probability of GCA or the urgency; visual loss in GCA is typically sudden and can occur without prior visual symptoms. The absence of visual loss defines the window in which urgent treatment can still protect sight.

symptoms.symptom_name: Proximal shoulder-girdle aching and morning stiffness (probable polymyalgia rheumatica overlap)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Polymyalgia rheumatica overlaps with GCA in a substantial proportion of patients; proximal shoulder-girdle stiffness supports the systemic arteritic picture in this constellation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hardened, prominent, nodular, exquisitely tender right superficial temporal artery with a diminished pulse compared with the left

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hardened, nodular, tender temporal artery with a diminished or absent pulse is the classic clinical examination hallmark of temporal arteritis and strongly supports the diagnosis. This clinician-obtained sign cannot be assessed over telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Markedly elevated inflammatory markers (ESR and CRP)

loinc_ref.display: C reactive protein [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

investigation_details.expected_result: Markedly elevated ESR and CRP consistent with active large-vessel inflammation; supports the diagnosis of giant cell arteritis but a treatment decision should not await the result
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Inflammatory activity not objectively confirmed; however, treatment with high-dose steroids should still not be delayed to obtain the result given the threat to vision.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A markedly raised ESR/CRP is characteristic of active GCA and forms part of the classification/diagnostic assessment, though normal markers do not fully exclude it and treatment should not await results.

symptoms.symptom_name: Temporal artery biopsy showing granulomatous transmural inflammation with giant cells (or temporal artery ultrasound halo sign)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Temporal artery biopsy (and/or temporal artery ultrasound)
investigation_details.expected_result: Biopsy: granulomatous inflammation of the vessel wall with giant cells and intimal hyperplasia; ultrasound: non-compressible halo sign around the temporal artery — either confirms GCA
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Definitive confirmation not obtained; however, high-dose steroids must be started before biopsy, which remains informative for 1-2 weeks after steroid initiation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positive temporal artery biopsy (or characteristic ultrasound halo) is the definitive confirmatory finding for GCA, obtained in the urgent in-person pathway.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of a new localised temporal headache, jaw claudication, scalp tenderness, systemic inflammatory features, and proximal shoulder-girdle stiffness in an adult over 70 constitutes a classic giant cell arteritis constellation. Jaw claudication in particular carries a strong association with impending visual loss. The gestalt mandates urgent same-day assessment and immediate high-dose corticosteroids to protect sight, even though definitive confirmation requires clinician examination, inflammatory markers, and biopsy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line urgent blood tests to demonstrate the marked systemic inflammation typical of GCA. Cannot be performed via telehealth — arranged through the urgent same-day in-person pathway. Treatment must not be delayed to await the result.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Temporal artery biopsy (and/or temporal artery ultrasound)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required for definitive confirmation of GCA. Should be arranged urgently but must not delay initiation of high-dose corticosteroids, as biopsy remains informative for 1-2 weeks after steroids are started.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Temporal artery palpation and formal visual acuity/field and pupillary examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Essential first-line clinician examination to identify the abnormal temporal artery and to document baseline visual function. Cannot be performed over telehealth chat — requires urgent in-person assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine outpatient neuroimaging (e.g. elective CT/MRI brain) as the primary next step
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Routine outpatient imaging as the primary pathway is inappropriate here — it would delay the sight-protecting intervention. The priority is urgent same-day assessment, immediate high-dose corticosteroids, and urgent inflammatory markers, not deferred imaging.
